title: Simple XOR

content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers LLL and R (L+3≤R)R\: (L+3\leq R)R(L+3≤R). Output any **four distinct** integers between LLL and RRR (inclusive) such that their **bitwise `XOR`** is zero.

More formally, output any **four** integers a1,a2,a3,a4a_1, a_2, a_3, a_4a1​,a2​,a3​,a4​ such that:


a1⊕a2⊕a3⊕a4=0a_1\oplus a_2\oplus a_3\oplus a_4 = 0a1​⊕a2​⊕a3​⊕a4​=0
L≤a1,a2,a3,a4≤RL\leq a_1, a_2, a_3, a_4\leq RL≤a1​,a2​,a3​,a4​≤R
ai=aja_i=a_jai​=aj​ if and only if i=ji=ji=j
If more than one such quadruple exists, you may output **any** of them. If no such quadruple exists, print −1-1−1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers L,RL, RL,R.


Output Format
For each testcase, output any **four distinct** integers between LLL and RRR such that their **bitwise `XOR`** is zero, or output −1-1−1 in case no such quadruple of four distinct integers exists.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers L and R.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output four distinct integers a1 a2 a3 a4 (separated by spaces) such that L ≤ a_i ≤ R and a1⊕a2⊕a3⊕a4 = 0, or output -1 if no such quadruple exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case no even number e with e+3 ≤ 4 exists, so output -1. For the second test case e=2 works, giving 2 3 4 5 whose XOR is 0.
The smallest even number ≥5 is 6, but 6+3=9>7, therefore no quadruple exists.
e=10 is even and 10+3=13≤13, so 10 11 12 13 works (XOR = 0).

solution.approach: The XOR of four consecutive numbers (e, e+1, e+2, e+3) is always zero because e⊕(e+1)⊕(e+2)⊕(e+3)=0 for any integer e. Therefore we only need to find an even e inside [L, R] such that e+3 ≤ R. The smallest even number not smaller than L is e = (L is even) ? L : L+1. If e+3 ≤ R we output e, e+1, e+2, e+3; otherwise no suitable quadruple exists and we output -1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) besides input and output buffers.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();while(T-- >0){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());long L=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());long R=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());long e=(L%2==0)?L:L+1;if(e+3<=R){sb.append(e).append(' ').append(e+1).append(' ').append(e+2).append(' ').append(e+3);}else{sb.append(-1);}if(T>=0)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
